goal: Make the Upright/Reversed orientation label on the reading result readable (WCAG AA)
done: Scoped a deeper gold (#8d6a14, ~5:1) to body[data-page='reading'] .spread-orientation instead of --mt-gold-deep (#d49a2c, ~2.5:1), and eased letter-spacing 0.3em->0.22em. css/reading.css only.
symptom: The gold UPRIGHT/REVERSED tag under each card on the reading result was very faint / hard to read on the near-white card surface.
root_cause: color: var(--mt-gold-deep) = #d49a2c on rgba(255,255,255,0.9) is ~2.48:1 contrast, well below the 4.5:1 WCAG AA minimum for small text; it was the worst-contrast text on the result page (22 elements flagged in a full scan).
fix: Override the color to #8d6a14 scoped to .spread-orientation (not the shared --mt-gold-deep token, so other gold accents are unchanged); ease the heavy 0.3em tracking to 0.22em for legibility.
verified: chrome-devtools-mcp on edited file: computed color rgb(141,106,20), contrast 4.99:1 on white, AA pass
screenshot: still reads as gold, clearer
affects: reading result orientation tag across modes (question/full)
filed: minor: .full-integration-box__source card names (~4.25:1) and footer 'Start ... Reading' links (~4.25:1) are just under AA — not addressed here
notes: Mirror sync (www/ios) deferred — pre-existing drift (BUG-015); web serves canonical.
